classes.label: Country
classes.description: Sovereign country or territory identified by country code.

classes.label: Province
classes.description: First-level administrative division identified within a country.

classes.label: City
classes.description: City identified by name within a province and country.

classes.label: Country Population Observation
classes.description: Year-specific population measurement for a country.

classes.label: Country Religion Share
classes.description: Country-specific occurrence of a religion with a percentage value.

classes.label: Country Continent Coverage
classes.description: Participation of a country in a continent with percentage coverage.

classes.label: Border
classes.description: Border relation between two countries with border length.

modeling_decisions: One-to-one extension tables Population, Economy, and Politics were folded into Country as data properties rather than promoted to standalone classes.

Connector tables with meaningful attributes were reified as classes: Border, CountryContinentCoverage, OrganizationMembership, CountryReligionShare, CountryEthnicGroupShare, CountryLanguageUsage, and the three population observation tables.

Religion and EthnicGroup were not modeled as standalone global classes because the schema provides no independent entity tables or foreign keys for them; their rows are country-specific and percentage-bearing.
No inverse object properties were added.
Several self-referential relationships were kept with direct identifier-column mappings and empty join_paths because the relevant foreign-key targets are carried in the same source row.
caveats: Column semantics for Lake.River and River.Mountains are not fully explicit from schema alone; labels were chosen conservatively.
The located table has no declared primary key and contains multiple nullable location targets; mappings therefore use separate properties with null-check conditions.
Self-referential tables such as Politics, Language, River, and mergesWith may require SQL aliasing in some downstream systems even though the abstract mapping draft keeps join_paths empty.
omissions: No abstract superclass hierarchy was introduced to avoid unsupported abstraction.
No direct Country-to-Continent or Country-to-Organization shortcut object properties were added because the connector tables carry extra attributes and were reified instead.
No standalone classes were created for country/province/city alternate-name rows because they behave as auxiliary literal-value tables.
